answer: Apple's fiscal year 2023 revenue was $364.83 billion. This included $119.58 billion in Q1, $90.75 billion in Q2, $85.78 billion in Q3, and $94.93 billion in Q4.

results.title: Apple reports fourth quarter results - Apple
results.snippet: # Apple reports fourth quarter results. iPhone revenue sets September quarter record. CUPERTINO, CALIFORNIA Apple today announced financial results for its fiscal 2023 fourth quarter ended September 30, 2023. The Company posted quarterly revenue of $89.5 billion, down 1 percent year over year, and quarterly earnings per diluted share of $1.46, up 13 percent year over year. “Today Apple is pleased to report a September quarter revenue record for iPhone and an all-time revenue record in Services,” said Tim Cook, Apple’s CEO. “Our active installed base of devices has again reached a new all-time high across all products and all geographic segments, thanks to the strength of our ecosystem and unparalleled customer loyalty,” said Luca Maestri, Apple’s CFO. “During the September quarter, our business performance drove double digit EPS growth and we returned nearly $25 billion to our shareholders, while continuing to invest in our long-term growth plans.”. Apple will provide live streaming of its Q4 2023 financial results conference call beginning at 2:00 p.m. PT on November 2, 2023 at apple.com/investor/earnings-call.

results.title: Apple Sales And Profits Analysis For FY 2023 — Top 10 Insights
results.snippet: Following are the top 10 insights from our analysis of Apple’s sales and profits across its offerings and operating segments for FY 2023:. Americas business.** Apple recorded $163 billion in sales from the Americas segment (including both North and South America), representing 42% of total sales. Europe business.** Apple reported $94 billion in sales from the Europe segment (which includes European countries, India, the Middle East, and Africa), representing 25% of total sales. Greater China business.** Apple generated $73 billion in sales from the greater China segment (which includes mainland China, Hong Kong, and Taiwan), accounting for 19% of total sales. Japan and rest of Asia Pacific business.** Apple generated $24 billion in sales from Japan (or 6% of total sales), a decline of 7% YoY. The rest of the Asia Pacific segment (which includes Australia and other Asian countries not covered by the other reportable segments) generated $30 billion in sales (or 8% of total sales), an increase by 1% YoY, and an operating margin of 41% in FY 2023.

results.title: Apple Revenue 2016-2025 - Bullfincher
results.snippet: Apple's quarterly revenue was **$124.30 B**(Q1: Dec 2024), **$95.36 B**(Q2: Mar 2025), **$94.04 B**(Q3: Jun 2025), **$102.47 B**(Q4: Sep 2025) in fiscal year 2025. Apple's quarterly revenue was **$119.58 B**(Q1: Dec 2023), **$90.75 B**(Q2: Mar 2024), **$85.78 B**(Q3: Jun 2024), **$94.93 B**(Q4: Sep 2024) in fiscal year 2024. Apple's quarterly revenue was **$117.15 B**(Q1: Dec 2022), **$94.84 B**(Q2: Apr 2023), **$81.80 B**(Q3: Jul 2023), **$89.50 B**(Q4: Sep 2023) in fiscal year 2023. Apple's quarterly revenue was **$123.95 B**(Q1: Dec 2021), **$97.28 B**(Q2: Mar 2022), **$82.96 B**(Q3: Jun 2022), **$90.15 B**(Q4: Sep 2022) in fiscal year 2022. Apple's quarterly revenue was **$111.44 B**(Q1: Dec 2020), **$89.58 B**(Q2: Mar 2021), **$81.43 B**(Q3: Jun 2021), **$83.36 B**(Q4: Sep 2021) in fiscal year 2021. Apple's quarterly revenue was **$91.82 B**(Q1: Dec 2019), **$58.31 B**(Q2: Mar 2020), **$59.69 B**(Q3: Jun 2020), **$64.70 B**(Q4: Sep 2020) in fiscal year 2020. Apple's quarterly revenue was **$78.35 B**(Q1: Dec 2016), **$52.90 B**(Q2: Apr 2017), **$45.41 B**(Q3: Jul 2017), **$52.58 B**(Q4: Sep 2017) in fiscal year 2017.
